name: Nemotron 3 Nano Omni
archDisplay: Hybrid Mamba-2/Transformer MoE, omni-modal (23 Mamba-2 layers + 23 MoE layers of 128 experts + 6 GQA layers), 30B total / 3B active
note: First truly omni-modal Nemotron 3 model (text/vision/audio/video natively unified, not vision-language with audio bolted on); smallest member of the family alongside Nemotron 3 Super and Ultra (already in corpus).

results.notes: Note: this is actually the Artificial Analysis composite Intelligence Index score (15), not a GPQA-specific score -- included here only because no single-benchmark independent number could be verified; treat with caution. [Corrected during full-corpus audit: The existing entry mapped the Artificial Analysis composite Intelligence Index score (15) to the 'gpqa' slug, which the corpus's own verificationNotes flagged as not actually a GPQA score. The NVIDIA arXiv technical report (Table 10, 'Comparison of Nemotron 3 Nano Omni, Nemotron 3 Nano LLM, and Qwen3-Omni across selected text-only benchmarks') reports the model's real GPQA-Diamond (no-tools, 4-run]
Table 10 of the arXiv technical report.
Table 10; AIME25, no tools, Pass@1 average of 8 runs.
Table 7, MMMU (val), reasoning-on mode (matches corpus's 'reasoning' effortLabel config); reasoning-off scores 55.2.
Table 7, MathVista-Mini split, reasoning-on mode; corroborated by Table 11's reasoning-budget ablation (82.8 with budget vs 80.3 without).
Table 7, ChartQA Test split, reasoning-on mode.
Table 7, DocVQA Test split, reasoning-on mode; a separate Val-split ablation in Table 11 scores 95.2-95.3.
Table 7, AI2D Test split; identical score in both reasoning-on and reasoning-off modes.

Table 7, CharXiv reasoning (RQ) split, reasoning-on mode; independently corroborated by NVIDIA's HF launch blog (huggingface.co/blog/nvidia/nemotron-3-nano-omni-multimodal-intelligence) which states the identical figure.
Table 7, reasoning-on mode; a reasoning-budget ablation in Table 11 gives a nearly identical 90.6.
Table 7, VideoMME (w/o subtitles), reasoning-on mode; independently corroborated by NVIDIA's HF launch blog stating the identical figure.

OCRBench (Test), reasoning-on mode (matches the corpus's tracked config), from Table 7 of the official NVIDIA technical report, confirmed via a raw pdftotext -layout extraction of the report PDF (mirrored at research.nvidia.com/labs/nemotron/files/NVIDIA-Nemotron-3-Omni-report.pdf) to avoid image-OCR misreads: 'OCRBench 88.3 86.6' (reasoning-off / reasoning-on). Distinct from the already-tracked 'OCRBenchV2 (EN/ZH)' row, which is a different, harder benchmark version not in the taxonomy. [score corrected during round-6 completeness verify: proposed 86.6 -> 866] [score corrected during round-12 accuracy re-verify: 866 -> 86.6, per verifier: Downloaded and pdftotext'd the actual arXiv PDF (2604.24954v2, verified genuine via arXiv export API). Table 7 reads exactly 'OCRBench 88.3 86.6' (reasoning-off / reasoning-on). Source reports 86.6 for reasoning-on, not 866. The corpus note itself ad]
TextVQA (Val), reasoning-on mode, from Table 7 of the NVIDIA technical report, confirmed via pdftotext -layout extraction: 'TextVQA (Val) 85.1 81.0' (reasoning-off / reasoning-on).

OSWorld (agentic computer-use benchmark), reasoning-on mode, from Table 7 of the NVIDIA technical report, confirmed via pdftotext -layout extraction: 'OSWorld - 47.4' (reasoning-off not reported / reasoning-on = 47.4). Only the reasoning-on figure exists for this model.

Humanity's Last Exam score from Artificial Analysis's independent Intelligence Index v4.1 evaluation, position-verified against the full 28-model HLE ranking list on the model's AA page ('Nemotron 3 Nano Omni 30B A3B Reasoning' = 5%). The existing corpus 'gpqa' entry (72.2, self-reported) already covers GPQA so AA's independent GPQA re-run (47%) was deliberately skipped as a duplicate slug per instructions; HLE was not previously tracked at all.

MMMU-Pro (harder variant of MMMU, distinct taxonomy slug from the corpus's existing 'mmmu' result of 70.8) from Artificial Analysis's independent evaluation, position-verified against the 17-model MMMU-Pro ranking list ('Nemotron 3 Nano Omni 30B A3B Reasoning' = 53%, second-lowest of the models AA has scored on this eval).

verificationNotes: Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index score of 15 (#29/130 in class) is an independent re-run; no independently-verified scores found for the individual named benchmarks (MMlongbench-Doc, OCRBenchV2, WorldSense, DailyOmni, VoiceBench) that NVIDIA's own materials claim leadership on, so only the composite index is reported.
